description:
## Background and intent

This bead joins the static shell and the data-loading service into the first complete user-visible Issue List slice. At the end of this task, Beadsmith should open to the issue explorer, read All Issues from the Current Workspace through the approved Rust/TauRPC/Effect path, and render those summaries in the middle pane while preserving honest loading, empty, and error states.

This is still not the full product. Search, sidebar filters, workspace switching, issue selection, and issue detail behavior remain future work. The important user value here is that the app finally shows real Beadwork issue data in the intended shell without becoming a second issue tracker.

## What to build

Wire the three-pane issue explorer shell from `bsm-tes` to the Effect issue-summary service from `bsm-mq4.3`. Render real `IssueSummary` rows in the middle pane and display the returned `workspacePath` as read-only context for the Current Workspace.

The UI should distinguish loading, true empty, and backend/error states while keeping the shell mounted. Empty means the RPC succeeded and returned no issues. Error means the app could not read issues, such as missing `bw`, invalid workspace, subprocess failure, or parse/contract failure. Do not paper over errors by showing an empty list.

Issue rows should be dense and scannable: id, title, status/priority/type cues, and useful dependency/label metadata where space allows. Preserve accessibility and stable layout. If an issue row is clickable-looking for styling reasons, it must not imply detail behavior unless that behavior is implemented; prefer inert styling or disabled affordances for this slice.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The issue list renders real data from the Effect issue-summary service.
- [ ] The UI displays the returned `workspacePath` as read-only Current Workspace text.
- [ ] Loading state is visible and keeps the three-pane shell mounted.
- [ ] Empty output renders a true empty state, not an error.
- [ ] Backend read failures render a visible error state while preserving the app shell.
- [ ] Search and sidebar filters remain visible but inert or disabled.
- [ ] The right pane remains in the `No issue selected` empty state; clicking an issue does not implement detail behavior.
- [ ] React components consume the Effect service rather than calling raw Tauri APIs directly.
- [ ] Rendered rows use stable keys from issue ids and handle absent optional fields without broken text.
- [ ] Focus, contrast, and text overflow are acceptable at the target desktop viewport.
- [ ] Relevant frontend formatting/check/test/build commands pass.

## Out of scope

- Workspace switching or saved workspace settings.
- Implemented search/filter behavior.
- Issue selection, issue detail loading, editing, comments, or mutations.
- Local caching or a separate issue store.

## Triage

ready-for-agent

comments.text: Planning audit: this bead is the first complete UI data join, not a scope expansion into full issue management. It should render real summaries and workspace context while keeping search, filters, selection, and details visibly out of scope. Preserve the shell for loading, empty, and error states.

title: Render real IssueSummary rows in the issue explorer
